classification_rationale: This video is primarily educational/instructional content. It shows: (1) conference/exhibition setting with multiple presenters and trainees examining FLS simulator equipment, (2) narrated Chinese text overlay describing the competition and training purpose, (3) close-up demonstration of peg transfer task being performed, (4) multiple angles of the simulator and trainee hands performing the task, (5) audience members observing and learning. The presence of narration, educational context (training academy competition), multiple demonstrators, and learning environment clearly indicate this is instructional rather than a competitive performance being scored.

frame_analyses.description: Wide shot of laparoscopic surgery training conference/exhibition. Multiple attendees with ID badges observing FLS simulator stations.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Equipment setup visible; station 1 marked with number; professional medical conference environment

frame_analyses.description: Close-up of trainee hands at peg transfer simulator with blue instrument handles visible. Foam pegs and transfer field visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrator grasping instruments with both hands; positioned at simulator console

frame_analyses.description: Trainee performing peg transfer task. Both hands holding instruments (blue handles); pegs visible in field; monitor display shows laparoscopic camera view.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Two-handed instrument control demonstrated; proper posture at simulator

frame_analyses.description: Close-up of hands transferring peg between pegs. Right hand (dominant) grasping, left hand assisting. Monitor shows internal camera feed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Coordinated bilateral hand movement; smooth instrument handling visible

frame_analyses.description: Wide view showing trainee at simulator with audience members observing from multiple angles. Screens display laparoscopic feed.
Trainee continues peg transfer movements. Multiple monitors visible showing different angles of task performance.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Continuous task execution with consistent technique

confidence_rationale: Video classification as instructional is very high confidence. Clear visual evidence of educational/conference setting, Chinese narration, multiple presenters/observers, and teaching context. Task identification (Peg Transfer) is certain from visible simulator setup and foam pegs. However, this is NOT a scoreable performance video—it is educational content documenting a training competition event. No individual performance score should be computed.
cannot_determine: Actual completion time—video is edited montage of conference footage, not continuous task performance recording
Specific penalties—video does not show complete uninterrupted task execution for penalty assessment
Individual trainee performance metrics—video focuses on equipment demonstration and educational context rather than single trainee performance
Task completion time and FLS score—this is instructional content, not a formal performance evaluation

demonstrated_techniques: Bilateral hand coordination at peg transfer simulator
Proper instrument handling with blue-handled graspers
Laparoscopic camera feed integration with task performance
verbal_cues: Chinese narration: '中国医师协会腹腔镜外科医师专业委员会' (Chinese Medical Association Laparoscopic Surgery Committee)
References to first national laparoscopic surgery skills competition
Training academy purpose and physician skill development emphasis
Regional competition structure mentioned (Northeast region, Northwest region, North China region, South China region, etc.)
common_errors_addressed: Video demonstrates proper two-handed instrument control—avoiding excessive single-hand dominance
Shows coordinated peg transfer technique without dropping objects
Demonstrates proper positioning at simulator to maintain task field visibility

video_purpose: Educational documentation of Chinese Medical Association laparoscopic surgery skills competition. Filmed as conference/exhibition footage showing trainee demonstration, equipment, and training purpose. Not a formal individual performance evaluation.
recommendation: DO NOT ASSIGN FLS SCORE. This is instructional/documentary content. If you require scoring of individual trainee performance, request unedited continuous video of a single trainee completing the task in a formal testing environment.
